'''Madmartigan''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[knight]] from [[Andowyne]]. The knight of Galadoorn disgraced himself by having an illicit love affair with a [[Prince/Legends|princess]]. He later took up a sword to fight [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] Bavmorda alongside his childhood friend Airk Thaughbaer. Madmartigan would vanish a year later when the Cataclysm destroyed [[Tir Asleen]].<ref name="Databank" />

==Behind the scenes==
Early in the production of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']], [[George Lucas]] wanted the design of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] to have a warrior look reminiscent of [[Willow (film)|''Willow'']]'s Madmartigan.<ref name="TAoSWEI">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace]]''</ref>

As an [[Wikipedia:April Fools' Day|April Fools' joke]] for [[April 1]], [[2006]], [[StarWars.com]] updated its [[Databank (original)|Databank]] with elements from the film ''Willow'' and its spin-off novels, written as if they were part of the ''[[Star Wars]]'' universe. The joke later was revealed, making the ''Willow'' entries non-[[canon]].<ref name="DB news">{{DB|updates|401/news20060401.html|The Shadow War Chronicles}}</ref>

The two movie franchises appear together in the non-canonical 1993 ''[[George Lucas' Super Live Adventure]]''.<ref name="Super Live Adventure">''[[George Lucas' Super Live Adventure]]''</ref>
